title: buildah login and logout, do not need to enter user namespace
body:
Currently we are entering the user namespace to be able to login
and logout, since this is not required, we should not enter.

This also would allow these commands to execute within a docker
container by default.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3037

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

@giuseppe do you know where this is covered in podman?  IE can we get podman login/logout to not happen within the user namespace?

> @giuseppe do you know where this is covered in podman? IE can we get podman login/logout to not happen within the user namespace?

we tag each command that needs the userns with `registry.UnshareNSRequired`
The only command I see this with is Mount?  Does this mean all other podman commands enter the user namespace?
